root_text.ud4.3:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosalesu cārikaṁ carati mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ.
root_text.ud4.3:1.3: Atha kho bhagavā maggā okkamma yena aññataraṁ rukkhamūlaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.ud4.3:2.1: Atha kho aññataro gopālako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.ud4.3:2.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho taṁ gopālakaṁ bhagavā dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi.
root_text.ud4.3:3.1: Atha kho so gopālako bhagavatā dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud4.3:3.2: “adhivāsetu me, bhante, bhagavā svātanāya bhattaṁ saddhiṁ bhikkhusaṅghenā”ti.
root_text.ud4.3:3.4: Atha kho so gopālako bhagavato adhivāsanaṁ viditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi.
root_text.ud4.3:4.1: Atha kho so gopālako tassā rattiyā accayena sake nivesane pahūtaṁ appodakapāyāsaṁ paṭiyādāpetvā navañca sappiṁ bhagavato kālaṁ ārocesi:
root_text.ud4.3:5.1: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya saddhiṁ bhikkhusaṅghena yena tassa gopālakassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.ud4.3:5.2: Atha kho so gopālako buddhappamukhaṁ bhikkhusaṅghaṁ appodakapāyāsena navena ca sappinā sahatthā santappesi sampavāresi.
root_text.ud4.3:5.3: Atha kho so gopālako bhagavantaṁ bhuttāviṁ onītapattapāṇiṁ aññataraṁ nīcaṁ āsanaṁ gahetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.ud4.3:5.4: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho taṁ gopālakaṁ bhagavā dhammiyā kathāya sandassetvā samādapetvā samuttejetvā sampahaṁsetvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkāmi.
root_text.ud4.3:5.5: Atha kho acirapakkantassa bhagavato taṁ gopālakaṁ aññataro puriso sīmantarikāya jīvitā voropesi.
root_text.ud4.3:6.1: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.ud4.3:6.2: “yena, bhante, gopālakena ajja buddhappamukho bhikkhusaṅgho appodakapāyāsena navena ca sappinā sahatthā santappito sampavārito so kira, bhante, gopālako aññatarena purisena sīmantarikāya jīvitā voropito”ti.
root_text.ud4.3:7.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud4.3:1.2: At one time the Buddha was wandering in the land of the Kosalans together with a large Saṅgha of mendicants.
translation_text.ud4.3:1.3: And then the Buddha left the road, went to the root of a certain tree, and sat down on the seat spread out.
translation_text.ud4.3:2.1: Then a certain cowherd went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side.
translation_text.ud4.3:2.2: The Buddha educated, encouraged, fired up, and inspired him with a Dhamma talk.
translation_text.ud4.3:3.1: Then the cowherd said to the Buddha,
translation_text.ud4.3:3.2: “Sir, would the Buddha, together with the mendicant Saṅgha, please accept tomorrow’s meal from me?”
translation_text.ud4.3:3.4: Then, knowing that the Buddha had consented, the cowherd got up from his seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on his right, before leaving.
translation_text.ud4.3:4.1: And when the night had passed the cowherd had plenty of thick milk-rice prepared in his own home, with fresh ghee. Then he had the Buddha informed of the time, saying,
translation_text.ud4.3:4.2: “Sir, it’s time. The meal is ready.”
translation_text.ud4.3:5.1: The Buddha robed up, took his bowl and robe and, together with the Sangha of monks, went to the house of that cowherd, where he sat down on the prepared seat in the dining hall.
translation_text.ud4.3:5.2: Then the cowherd served and satisfied the mendicant Saṅgha headed by the Buddha with his own hands with a thick milk-rice and fresh ghee.
translation_text.ud4.3:5.3: When the Buddha had eaten and washed his hand and bowl, the cowherd took a low seat and sat to one side.
translation_text.ud4.3:5.4: The Buddha then instructed, inspired, and gladdened him with a teaching, after which he got up and left.
translation_text.ud4.3:5.5: But soon after the Buddha had left, the cowherd was killed by a certain man in the gap between village perimeters.
translation_text.ud4.3:6.1: Then several mendicants went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened.
translation_text.ud4.3:7.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud4.3:8.3: than a hater to the hated,
translation_text.ud4.3:8.4: or an enemy to their foe.”
